subject: Statistics
title: Moving Averages
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Moving Averages.

hook.text: Stock traders use the 50-day moving average like a compass. When the price crosses it, they buy or sell.

buildup.text: A moving average replaces each data point with the average of its neighbors, smoothing out short-term noise.

discovery.text: The window size controls the smoothness. A 3-point average is lightly smoothed. A 30-point average is heavily smoothed.

twist.text: Moving averages lag behind the data. They react slowly to sudden changes — the smoothing is also a delay.

climax.text: Centered moving averages are used for decomposition. Trailing averages are used for real-time signals.

punchline.text: Average the neighbors. The noise fades. The signal emerges. But it arrives late.

quiz.question: What is the main trade-off when using moving averages?
options: Smoothing removes noise but introduces a lag — the signal is delayed
Larger windows make the data noisier
Moving averages only work on financial data
